goodwin-vikki.text: On education, Vikki Goodwin's recorded position: Rep. Goodwin strongly opposes school voucher programs, calling them a 'terrible deal for Texas kids' that diverts billions from public education to unaccountable private schools. She supports raising teacher pay, reducing standardized testing, and keeping public education dollars in public schools.

mccormick-kevin.text: No public data in our set connects Kevin McCormick's recorded background or positions specifically to property taxes or public school funding.
No public data in our set on Kevin McCormick's recorded votes or stated positions regarding property taxes or public school funding.

patrick-dan.text: On taxes, Dan Patrick's recorded position: Dan Patrick has made property tax reduction a central priority, championing increases to the homestead exemption from $15,000 to $100,000 (now $140,000), with higher amounts for seniors. He supports constitutional amendments to make these permanent and has pushed for lowering rollback rates to restrict local government revenue growth.

On education, Dan Patrick's recorded position: Dan Patrick has made school choice his signature issue, successfully passing SB 2 in 2025 to establish universal Education Savings Accounts (ESAs) for up to 100,000 Texas students. He has prioritized this issue for over a decade, with the Texas Senate passing school choice bills six times under his leadership. The program prioritizes students with disabilities and lower-income families but is universally available.

caveats: We only show a connection to homeowner parent concerns when a candidate's own recorded background in our data set names it directly (for example, a farming or small-business background). Where no such record exists, we say so rather than guess at a candidate's likely position.
summary: For a homeowner parent voter looking at the Lieutenant Governor of Texas race (Dan Patrick, Vikki Goodwin, Mike Collier, Anthony Cristo, Kevin McCormick): our data set does not contain candidate-specific positions or voting records on property taxes or public school funding for this race. Below is what we do have on each candidate's background and, where available, campaign finance.
